page.title: Opioid Use Disorder — RepurpOS | OpenSourceMedicine
page.description: Structured alterations and therapeutics for Opioid Use Disorder: molecular targets, clinical biomarkers, phenotypes, and ranked drugs from Open Targets, HPO, ChEMBL, and DGIdb.
page.hero: Disease intelligence for Opioid Use Disorder: 107 alterations and 289 ranked therapeutics from curated public databases.

Natural agents are candidates from OSMF narrative reviews on opensourcemed.info/chronic-disease.html — not approved treatments. This page is for research orientation only — not medical advice.

remission.spontaneous_remission_rate: Spontaneous recovery (without MOUD) in 20-30% of OUD patients over 10 years; but overdose mortality risk during active disorder is extreme (5-10 per 1000 person-years); relapse rate 90%+ in first year after discontinuing MOUD
remission.best_intervention_remission_rate: Buprenorphine maintenance: 50-70% retention in treatment at 1 year vs <20% for placebo; naltrexone extended-release (Vivitrol): comparable to buprenorphine when initiated; methadone: superior retention but restricted distribution; overdose mortality reduced 50-70% with any MOUD
remission.gap_size: Very large — only 20% of people with OUD receive MOUD; massive unmet need
remission.barrier_detail: US eliminated the DEA X-waiver requirement for buprenorphine prescribing in December 2022 (a major policy win) but prescribing rates have increased slowly; stigma remains; methadone access restricted to OTPs (clinics); harm reduction infrastructure (fentanyl test strips, naloxone) still insufficiently deployed; 80% of people with OUD receive no treatment
remission.last_soc_change: 2023 — DEA X-waiver elimination fully implemented; 2022 federal legislation allowing take-home methadone expanded; 2021 FDA approved injectable buprenorphine (Sublocade) for once-monthly dosing; buprenorphine SL since 2002; methadone for OUD since 1964
remission.outdated_treatments: Abstinence-only treatment (detoxification without MOUD initiation — associated with 5-10x increased overdose mortality risk in first month post-discharge; widely practiced in many US facilities); opioid agonist therapy tapering (MOUD is most effective as indefinite maintenance, not tapered); 28-day treatment programs without MOUD transition
remission.adjunctive_emerging: Naloxone co-prescribing with any opioid prescription (expanding); fentanyl test strips (harm reduction); supervised consumption sites (SCS) — operating in New York since 2021; injectable extended-release naltrexone (CAM2038); contingency management (voucher-based reinforcement — highly effective but implementation barriers)
remission.frontier_signal: Long-acting buprenorphine implants (Probuphine — 6-month duration); buprenorphine-naltrexone combination approaches; naloxone higher-dose formulations (8mg nasal spray Kloxxado for fentanyl analog reversal); fentanyl vaccines in preclinical development; AI-based overdose prediction and prevention platforms
remission.notes: Manually curated from landmark trials, registries, and guidelines (disease_db_100.json). Protected from automated overwrite.

burden.note: Tier from NIH FY funding ÷ US DALYs (or mortality proxy) vs corpus quartiles
